flashcards.back: Memory management technique that uses disk storage as an extension of RAM, allowing processes to use larger address spaces than physical memory.

flashcards.front: What problem does virtual memory solve?
flashcards.back: 1. Run processes larger than physical RAM 2. Enable efficient memory sharing 3. Provide memory protection between processes
Data structure mapping virtual addresses to physical addresses, tracking page locations in memory/disk.
Translation Lookaside Buffer: Hardware cache storing recent virtual-to-physical address translations to speed up memory access.
When a process accesses a page not currently loaded in physical memory.
Loading pages into memory only when needed, reducing initial memory footprint.
Performance degradation when system spends more time swapping pages than executing processes.

flashcards.front: What is the working set model?
flashcards.back: Set of pages a process needs in memory during a time window to prevent thrashing.
Segmentation divides memory into logical units, paging uses fixed-size physical blocks.
Memory optimization where processes share pages until one modifies them.
